channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ricky Stuart maintains stance on NRL refereeing concerns after Raiders loss
item.description: Canberra Raiders coach Ricky Stuart has reaffirmed his concerns about the consistency of NRL officiating following his side’s 30-22 defeat to the Dolphins, stating that a detailed review of the match ...

item.title: Ugandan Health Ministry Advises Ebola Survivors on Post-Recovery Precautions
item.description: The Ministry of Health in Uganda has issued guidance for men who have recovered from Ebola, advising them to abstain from sexual activity for up to six months or use condoms consistently. Health exper...

item.title: DCI Arrests TikToker for Offensive Posts on Utumishi Girls' Dormitory Fire
item.description: The Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) has arrested David Onyango Elgon, popularly known as MC Adek Tatu, following public outrage over his social media posts perceived as insensitive after ...

item.title: Trump-linked festival struggles to replace performers after multiple withdrawals
item.description: A multi-day patriotic festival known as the “Great American State Fair,” organized by a group linked to former President Donald Trump under the Freedom 250 initiative, is reportedly facing significant...

item.title: Bulls secure URC semifinal spot with emphatic win over Munster
item.description: The Bulls put on a commanding performance at Loftus, overwhelming Munster 45-14 in the URC quarterfinals to book their place in the semifinals against Glasgow Warriors. From the outset, the Bulls’ bac...

item.title: Arsenal consider move for Khvicha Kvaratskhelia amid transfer speculation
item.description: Arsenal Football Club is being linked with a possible move for Napoli’s star winger Khvicha Kvaratskhelia, as reports suggest the London side is looking to strengthen its attacking options for the ong...

item.title: Sculpture of Lord Buddha by Sandhya Patnaik Unveiled at VIMS, Visakhapatnam
item.description: On Saturday, Visakha Institute of Medical Sciences (VIMS) in Visakhapatnam witnessed the unveiling of a remarkable sculpture of Lord Buddha, masterfully designed by the well-known artist Sandhya Patna...

item.title: KP Chief Minister requests GB Supreme Court to oversee free and fair elections
item.description: PESHAWAR: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Chief Minister Sohail Afridi has formally written to the Chief Justice of Gilgit-Baltistan Supreme Appellate Court, Sardar Muhammad Shamim Khan, seeking judicial oversight...

item.title: Retired Nigerian Military General and Wife Abducted in Katsina
item.description: Dem una report say Major General Rabe Abubakar wey don retire as Director of Defence Information, and him wife don get kidnapped for Katsina State, for Matazu Local Government Area. Dem say armed men ...

item.title: WestJet mandates English proficiency for French-speaking employees
item.description: WestJet is facing attention after reports that it requires its French-speaking employees to be proficient in English. The Canadian Press article published on May 30, 2026, outlines a policy decision b...

item.title: Tanzania names Bud Crawford as Tourism Ambassador to boost international awareness
item.description: Tanzania has officially appointed Terence “Bud” Crawford, the American boxing champion, as its new Tourism Ambassador. The announcement was made by the Minister for Natural Resources and Tourism, Dr. ...

item.title: Quebec Court Grants Conditional Discharge to Tunisian Immigrant for Domestic Assault
item.description: In Quebec, Mohamed Aziz Ben Ishak, a Tunisian national who arrived in Canada a year prior as a temporary resident, received a conditional discharge and three years of probation for a violent domestic ...

item.title: Jeffrey Sachs Urges German Chancellor Merz to Pursue Diplomacy with Russia to Prevent Escalation in Ukraine
item.description: Economist and diplomat Jeffrey Sachs has issued an urgent appeal to German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, urging immediate diplomatic engagement with Russian President Vladimir Putin to prevent a wider wa...

item.title: India’s information landscape: countering targeted social-media manipulation harming the growth narrative
item.description: The article discusses a coordinated information warfare campaign targeting India’s burgeoning growth story, particularly its digitally savvy youth. It explains how hostile networks, both domestic and ...

item.title: Federal Court backlog grows amid rising immigration applications and technology use
item.description: The number of immigration cases in Canada's Federal Court has surged significantly, more than quadrupling since 2020. Immigration lawyers attribute part of this increase to the federal government’s us...

item.title: Environmental destruction and violence against communities highlight urgent need to protect forests and livelihoods in the Philippines
item.description: The opinion article discusses the alarming destruction of trees across the Philippines and links it to broader social and environmental harms affecting communities. It highlights recent cases in Metro...

item.title: Judge orders removal of Trump name from Kennedy Center amid legal dispute over renaming
item.description: A United States federal judge has ruled that Donald Trump’s name must be removed from the Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts, concluding that the venue cannot be renamed without approval from Cong...

item.title: Dogs survive late rally to edge out Collingwood in a tense AFL showdown
item.description: The Western Bulldogs clung to a four-point victory over Collingwood in a dramatic AFL clash, thanks to a late-game heroics by Michael Sellwood. Sellwood, a rookie draftee in 2025, took a decisive pack...

item.title: Jigawa Police Arrest Suspects, Recover Stolen Motorcycles, Cattle, and Drugs
item.description: For Jigawa State, police don really show say dem dey serious against crime as dem don arrest plenty suspects wey dey involved in theft, cattle rustling, drug peddling, and other criminal wahala across...

item.title: Weekly Canadian business roundup: economy slows, bank profits rise, and Tim Hortons adjusts foreign hiring strategy
item.description: Canada’s economy showed signs of stagnation in the first quarter, contracting at an annualised rate of 0.1 per cent, according to Statistics Canada. This follows a 1 per cent annualised decline in the...
